agent_output: {"handoff_brief":{"recipient":"Shaq","task_context":"Implement the Order Fulfillment state management system. Bird has defined the domain invariants governing order state transitions and refund eligibility. MJ has designed the technical architecture: a PATCH endpoint for state changes, an OrderStateMachine for transition validation, and a separate RefundService that verifies order state before issuing refunds. Your job is to turn these specs into production-ready code with tests.","domain_rules":[{"rule":"An order cannot transition from SHIPPED to PENDING","testable_assertion":"Calling transition(SHIPPED, PENDING) on OrderStateMachine throws/returns ORDER_STATE_INVALID"},{"rule":"A refund can only be issued on a DELIVERED order","testable_assertion":"RefundService.issue(orderId) returns a refund-rejected error when the order state is anything other than DELIVERED"}],"architecture_guidance":[{"decision":"Expose a PATCH /orders/{id}/state endpoint for state transitions","implementation_note":"The endpoint receives the target state in the request body. Delegate all transition validation to OrderStateMachine before persisting. Return appropriate HTTP status codes: 200 on success, 409 Conflict with error code on invalid transitions."},{"decision":"State transitions validated in an OrderStateMachine class","implementation_note":"OrderStateMachine owns the transition graph. It should be a pure domain class with no infrastructure dependencies — takes current state and requested state, returns success or a domain error. This makes it trivially unit-testable."},{"decision":"Refund logic lives in a separate RefundService","implementation_note":"RefundService calls the order service (or repository) to read current order state and rejects the refund if state is not DELIVERED. Keep refund issuance and order state verification in a single transactional boundary or use an idempotency mechanism to avoid partial failures."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"criterion":"Invalid state transition is rejected","given":"An order in SHIPPED state","when":"A revert-to-pending (transition to PENDING) is attempted via PATCH /orders/{id}/state","then":"The request is rejected with HTTP 409 and error code ORDER_STATE_INVALID"},{"criterion":"Refund rejected on non-DELIVERED order","given":"An order in any state other than DELIVERED","when":"A refund is requested via RefundService","then":"The refund is rejected with an appropriate error indicating the order is not in DELIVERED state"},{"criterion":"Refund succeeds on DELIVERED order","given":"An order in DELIVERED state","when":"A refund is requested via RefundService","then":"The refund is issued successfully"},{"criterion":"Valid state transitions succeed","given":"An order in a state with a valid forward transition (e.g., PENDING to SHIPPED)","when":"The transition is requested via PATCH /orders/{id}/state","then":"The order state is updated and HTTP 200 is returned"}],"terminology_alignment":[{"domain_term":"Invariant","technical_term":"Validation rule / guard clause","definition":"A business rule that must always hold true. In code, these are enforced as guard clauses or validation checks in OrderStateMachine and RefundService that reject operations violating the rule."},{"domain_term":"State transition","technical_term":"PATCH /orders/{id}/state","definition":"A request to move an order from its current state to a new state. The domain models this as a directed graph of allowed transitions; the API exposes it as a PATCH endpoint."},{"domain_term":"ORDER_STATE_INVALID","technical_term":"409 Conflict response with error code","definition":"The domain error code returned when a requested state transition violates the transition graph. Mapped to HTTP 409 at the API layer."}],"contradictions_resolved":[],"open_questions":[]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":92,"high_confidence_areas":["Domain rules are explicit and testable","Architecture decisions are clear and non-conflicting","Acceptance criteria map directly from Bird's invariants"],"low_confidence_areas":["Full set of valid state transitions not enumerated — only the SHIPPED->PENDING prohibition is specified. Shaq may need to clarify the complete transition graph with Bird.","Refund success/failure response shape not specified"],"assumptions":["The complete set of order states includes at least PENDING, SHIPPED, and DELIVERED","OrderStateMachine is a pure domain class with no async or infrastructure dependencies","RefundService communicates with the order service in-process (not via network call) unless the architecture evolves"]}}

Bird has defined the domain invariants governing order state transitions and refund eligibility. MJ has designed the technical architecture: a PATCH endpoint for state changes, an OrderStateMachine for transition validation, and a separate RefundService that verifies order state before issuing refunds.
